loss of anterior vertebral body height compatible with discitis/osteomyelitis

tissue with trace T2 signal affecting the anterior inferior portion of the T5
vertebral body. Anterior epidural enhancement from T5-T7 suspicious for small
component of epidural abscess. There is exaggerated kyphotic angulation at
the T6/T7 level with a small amount of retropulsion focally contacting the
cord but without significant canal stenosis. Normal cord signal. Otherwise
with multilevel non-critical degenerative changes. Multilevel Tarlov cysts.

destruction with focal involvement of T5 and probable tiny epidural abscess
component from T5-T7 along with kyphotic angulation focally contacting the
spinal cord without compression. Normal cord signal.
